Control Arm - N.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) and Table 2 (Secondary End Points) both report 'ADT Alone (N=393)' as the number of participants in the control arm. This is confirmed in multiple locations, including the No. at Risk table in Figure 1A, which starts with 393 for the ADT alone group.

Control Arm.evidence: The methods section states that patients were randomly assigned to receive either 'ADT plus docetaxel' or 'ADT alone.' Throughout the results and tables (e.g., Table 1, Table 2), the comparison group is consistently referred to as 'ADT alone,' which is the arm that does not receive the experimental treatment (docetaxel) and serves as the control.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The Methods section states that patients were randomly assigned to either 'ADT alone' or 'ADT plus docetaxel,' indicating two arms: one experimental (ADT plus docetaxel) and one control (ADT alone). This is further confirmed by Table 1 and multiple references to the two groups throughout the document.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The methods section states, 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles.' Table 1 and multiple results sections consistently refer to these two arms: 'ADT plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone.'

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The median follow-up duration is explicitly stated in the Results section: 'After a median follow-up of 28.9 months, the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel...' and again in Figure 1 caption: 'The median duration of follow-up was 28.9 months among all patients (Panel A)...'. This value is for the whole study population.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The total number of participants included in the clinical trial is reported in multiple locations, including the Results section ('A total of 790 patients were enrolled and underwent randomization') and the forest plot for subgroups ('All patients | 790'). This number includes all participants randomized to either the ADT plus docetaxel group (N=397) or the ADT alone group (N=393), as confirmed in Table 1 and the No. at Risk tables in Figure 1.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The methods section states that patients were randomly assigned to receive either ADT plus docetaxel or ADT alone. The abstract and multiple sections throughout the document confirm that the first experimental arm (treatment arm 1) consisted of androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) combined with docetaxel, a cytotoxic chemotherapy agent.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life reported.evidence: There is no mention of quality of life outcomes, measurements, or results in the abstract, methods, results, tables, figures, or discussion. The endpoints and reported outcomes are limited to survival, PSA response, time to progression, and adverse events. Quality of life is not discussed or reported anywhere in the document.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) and Table 2 (Secondary End Points) both report 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' as the number of participants in the experimental arm. This is confirmed in the text: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles...' and 'From July 2006 through December 2012, a total of 790 patients were enrolled and underwent randomization.' The No. at Risk tables in Figure 1 also show 397 for the ADT+docetaxel group at baseline.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Results are reported for high-volume disease subgroups in Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Figure 1B (Kaplan-Meier survival for high-volume), and Figure 2 (hazard ratios for high-volume subgroup).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Results are reported for low-volume disease subgroups in Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Figure 1C (Kaplan-Meier survival for low-volume), and Figure 2 (hazard ratios for low-volume subgroup).

NCT.evidence: The ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT00309985, is explicitly stated in the CONCLUSIONS section: '(Funded by the National Cancer Institute and others; ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT00309985.)'

PubMed ID.evidence: The PubMed ID is not directly listed in the main text, but the citation 'N Engl J Med 2015;373:737-46. DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa1503747' allows lookup; PubMed ID 26244877 corresponds to this NEJM article.

Trial Name.evidence: The trial is referred to as the 'E3805 study' in the METHODS section: 'In this study, the E3805 study, patients received ADT alone or ADT plus docetaxel...'

Author.evidence: The author list is provided at the beginning of the article: 'Christopher J. Sweeney, M.B., B.S., Yu-Hui Chen, M.S., M.P.H., Michael Carducci, M.D., ... and Robert S. DiPaola, M.D.' Using 'et al.' is appropriate for brevity.

Year.evidence: The publication year is given in multiple places, e.g., 'This article was published on August 5, 2015, at NEJM.org.' and 'N Engl J Med 2015;373:737-46.'

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a full publication from the New England Journal of Medicine, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The study is described as a phase 3 trial in the discussion and references: '...across two phase 3 studies conducted in a similar time period.' and in reference 5: 'a SWOG phase III trial experience.'

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The article is an original report of the E3805 trial results, as indicated by the 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' heading and the description of primary results.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles, with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion. Daily prednisone was not required.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The regimen is described in the METHODS section: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles, with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion. Daily prednisone was not required.'

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document does not report any measure of quality of life, well-being, or satisfaction with health status as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey. There is no mention of quality of life scales or related results in the text, tables, or figures.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports the median age for the ADT plus Docetaxel (treatment) group as 64 years.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports the median age for the ADT Alone (control) group as 63 years.

Trial.evidence: The name 'E3805 study' is explicitly mentioned in the Methods section: 'In this study, the E3805 study, patients received ADT alone or ADT plus docetaxel at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer...' and 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.'

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The document does not report the number or percentage of patients with synchronous metastases (metastases at the time of diagnosis) in the treatment (ADT plus docetaxel) arm. Table 1 and the text provide data on volume of metastases (high/low), visceral metastases, and other baseline characteristics, but not on synchronous vs. metachronous presentation.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients with synchronous metastases (metastases at the time of diagnosis) in the control (ADT alone) arm. No table or text section provides this breakdown.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The document does not report the number or percentage of patients with metachronous metastases (metastases that occur after diagnosis) in the treatment (ADT plus docetaxel) arm. No such data are present in the baseline characteristics or elsewhere.

The document does not report the number or percentage of patients with metachronous metastases (metastases that occur after diagnosis) in the control (ADT alone) arm. No such data are present in the baseline characteristics or elsewhere.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: In the 'ASSIGNED THERAPY ADMINISTERED AND SUBSEQUENT THERAPY' section, it states 'approximately 86% of the 390 patients in the combination group who started the assigned therapy completed six cycles of docetaxel therapy (Table S2 in the Supplementary Appendix)'. This indicates that 390 out of 397 patients randomized to the ADT plus docetaxel group actually received docetaxel, which is 98%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In the same section, it states 'At the time of this analysis, castration-resistant prostate cancer with at least biochemical progression had developed in 287 patients assigned to ADT alone; 137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed.' This totals 147 patients in the control arm who received docetaxel before or during the trial, out of 393 randomized, which is approximately 37%.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival (time until death from any cause)
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The primary endpoint is explicitly stated in the Methods section: 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' Additionally, overall survival is defined as 'the time until death from any cause.'

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) reports for 'Volume of metastases — no. (%)' under 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)': High: 263 (66.2%).

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) reports for 'Volume of metastases — no. (%)' under 'ADT Alone (N=393)': High: 250 (63.6%).

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) reports for 'Volume of metastases — no. (%)' under 'ADT Alone (N=393)': Low: 143 (36.4%).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)' for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' the value for '0' as 277 (69.8%).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports under 'ECOG performance status — no. (%)' for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' the value for '0' as 272 (69.2%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 reports '1' as 114 (28.7%) and '2' as 6 (1.5%) for 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'. Summing these gives 120 (30.2%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Table 1 reports '1' as 115 (29.3%) and '2' as 6 (1.5%) for 'ADT Alone (N=393)'. Summing these gives 121 (30.8%).

Previous local therapy - N (%) | Prostat.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) lists 'Prostatectomy' under 'Prior treatment for prostate cancer — no. (%)' as 81 (20.4%) for the ADT plus Docetaxel (treatment) group.
Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) lists 'Prostatectomy' under 'Prior treatment for prostate cancer — no. (%)' as 73 (18.6%) for the ADT Alone (control) group.

Previous local therapy - N (%) | Orchiec.evidence: No value for previous orchiectomy as local therapy is reported in Table 1 or elsewhere in the document.
No value for previous orchiectomy as local therapy is reported in Table 1 or elsewhere in the document.

Previous local therapy - N (%) | Radioth.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) lists 'Primary radiation' under 'Prior treatment for prostate cancer — no. (%)' as 27 (6.8%) for the ADT plus Docetaxel (treatment) group.
Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics) lists 'Primary radiation' under 'Prior treatment for prostate cancer — no. (%)' as 33 (8.4%) for the ADT Alone (control) group.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: In the RESULTS section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months, with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The combination group refers to ADT plus docetaxel (treatment arm).

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: In the RESULTS section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months, with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The ADT-alone group is the control arm.

Metastases - N (%) | Liver | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of liver metastases specifically; only 'visceral metastases' as a combined category is reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Liver | Control.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of liver metastases specifically; only 'visceral metastases' as a combined category is reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Lungs | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of lung metastases specifically; only 'visceral metastases' as a combined category is reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Lungs | Control.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of lung metastases specifically; only 'visceral metastases' as a combined category is reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of bone metastases specifically; only 'volume of metastases' (high/low) and 'visceral metastases' are reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Control.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of bone metastases specifically; only 'volume of metastases' (high/low) and 'visceral metastases' are reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Nodal | Treatment.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of nodal metastases specifically; only 'volume of metastases' (high/low) and 'visceral metastases' are reported.

Metastases - N (%) | Nodal | Control.evidence: Table 1 and the rest of the document do not provide a breakdown of nodal metastases specifically; only 'volume of metastases' (high/low) and 'visceral metastases' are reported.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The secondary endpoints were: (1) proportion of patients with PSA level <0.2 ng/ml at 6 and 12 months (ADT plus docetaxel: 32.0% at 6 mo, 27.7% at 12 mo; ADT alone: 19.6% at 6 mo, 16.8% at 12 mo); (2) median time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (ADT plus docetaxel: 20.2 months [95% CI, 17.2-23.6], ADT alone: 11.7 months [95% CI, 10.8-14.7], HR 0.61 [0.51-0.72], P<0.001); (3) median time to clinical progression (ADT plus docetaxel: 33.0 months [95% CI, 27.3-41.2], ADT alone: 19.8 months [95% CI, 17.9-22.8], HR 0.61 [0.50-0.75], P<0.001).
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: Explicitly reported in the 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS' section and Table 2, which lists the secondary endpoints and their results for both treatment arms for the whole population.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: No table or text in the document reports the total number or percentage of patients experiencing all-cause grade 3 or higher adverse events in the treatment arm. Table 3 only reports treatment-related grade 3 or higher events for the docetaxel group.

The document explicitly states that adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, and no table or text provides all-cause grade 3 or higher adverse event rates for the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Table 3 reports 'Any event' for grade 3 (65 patients, 16.7%) and grade 4 (49 patients, 12.6%) among 390 patients who received docetaxel and had follow-up data. Summing these gives 114 patients (29.2%) with treatment-related grade 3 or higher adverse events in the treatment arm.

The document states that adverse events in the ADT-alone group were not routinely documented, and no table or text provides treatment-related grade 3 or higher adverse event rates for the control arm.
Table 3, under 'Any event', lists 1 patient (0.3%) with a grade 5 (treatment-related) adverse event in the docetaxel group.
No grade 5 treatment-related adverse events are reported for the control arm; adverse events were not routinely documented for this group.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: No data on overall response rate (ORR: complete or partial response) for the experimental arm is reported in the document. The only response-related data are PSA declines, not RECIST or ORR.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | CR.evidence: No data on complete response (CR) rate for the experimental arm is reported. The only response-related data are PSA declines, not RECIST or CR.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | SD.evidence: No data on stable disease (SD) rate for the experimental arm is reported. The document does not provide RECIST-based SD rates.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | PD.evidence: No data on progressive disease (PD) rate for the experimental arm is reported. The document does not provide RECIST-based PD rates.

ORR - N (%) | Control | Overall.evidence: No data on overall response rate (ORR: complete or partial response) for the control arm is reported in the document. The only response-related data are PSA declines, not RECIST or ORR.

ORR - N (%) | Control | CR.evidence: No data on complete response (CR) rate for the control arm is reported. The only response-related data are PSA declines, not RECIST or CR.

ORR - N (%) | Control | SD.evidence: No data on stable disease (SD) rate for the control arm is reported. The document does not provide RECIST-based SD rates.

ORR - N (%) | Control | PD.evidence: No data on progressive disease (PD) rate for the control arm is reported. The document does not provide RECIST-based PD rates.

Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' the row 'Black' as 37 (9.4%).

Race - N (%) | Asian | Control.evidence: Table 1 does not report a separate row for Asian; the only categories are White, Black, Other, and Unknown. Figure 2 footnote mentions Asian (8 patients) are included in 'Other or unknown', but no arm-wise breakdown is given.

Race - N (%) | Other | Control.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline Characteristics of the Patients) reports for 'ADT Alone (N=393)' the row 'Other' as 6 (1.5%).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 (Secondary End Points) reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer — mo' (which is the study's definition of progression-free survival) as median 20.2 months for ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397), which is the experimental arm.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Secondary End Points) reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer — mo' as median 11.7 months for ADT Alone (N=393), which is the control arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Hazard ratio for time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (PFS proxy): 0.61 (95% CI, 0.51-0.72); Median time to progression: 20.2 months (95% CI, 17.2-23.6)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' (a PFS proxy) for all patients: median 20.2 months (ADT plus docetaxel), hazard ratio 0.61 (95% CI, 0.51-0.72). No PFS rate as a percentage is reported, but the hazard ratio and median time are provided for the overall treatment arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Hazard ratio for time to castration-resistant prostate cancer (PFS proxy): 0.61 (95% CI, 0.51-0.72) (reference arm); Median time to progression: 11.7 months (95% CI, 10.8-14.7)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 reports 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer' (a PFS proxy) for all patients: median 11.7 months (ADT alone). The hazard ratio is for the comparison (treatment vs. control), so the control arm's value is the reference. No PFS rate as a percentage is reported, but the median time is provided for the overall control arm.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or ADT plus docetaxel (75 mg per square meter every 3 weeks for six cycles, with premedication with dexamethasone). Daily prednisone was not required.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The Methods section and the Abstract clearly state that participants were randomized to receive either ADT alone or ADT plus docetaxel (75 mg/m2 every 3 weeks for six cycles, with dexamethasone premedication). Daily prednisone was not required. This description is consistent throughout the document and applies to the whole study population.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document provides detailed results of the randomized controlled trial, including efficacy and safety outcomes, but does not explicitly report an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) for the RCT. There is no section or table summarizing the risk of bias or overall risk judgment for the trial according to standard COE frameworks.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The abstract and results section state: 'the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone (57.6 months vs. 44.0 months)'. Figure 1A and the corresponding text confirm this value for the overall treatment group.

Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: The abstract and results section state: 'the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone (57.6 months vs. 44.0 months)'. Figure 1A and the corresponding text confirm this value for the overall control group.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: Figure 1B and the results section state: 'the median overall survival that was 17.0 months longer in the combination group than in the ADT-alone group (49.2 months vs. 32.2 months)' for patients with high-volume disease.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Figure 1B and the results section state: 'the median overall survival that was 17.0 months longer in the combination group than in the ADT-alone group (49.2 months vs. 32.2 months)' for patients with high-volume disease.

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Figure 1C and the results section state: 'the median survival at the time of the analysis had not been reached in the subgroup with low-volume disease in either study group.'

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Figure 1C and the results section state: 'the median survival at the time of the analysis had not been reached in the subgroup with low-volume disease in either study group.'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The document repeatedly states that the clinical trial compared androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone versus ADT plus docetaxel. For example, in the abstract: 'We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' The methods section specifies: 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles.' Thus, the add-on treatment given in combination with the primary treatment (ADT) is docetaxel.

Treatment Class.value: chemohormonal therapy (androgen-deprivation therapy [ADT] plus docetaxel) versus ADT alone
Treatment Class.evidence: The document repeatedly describes the trial as a comparison between androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone and ADT plus docetaxel (chemohormonal therapy). For example, the abstract states, 'We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' The methods section further details randomization to 'ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel.' Thus, the treatment classes are chemohormonal therapy (ADT plus docetaxel) and ADT alone.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median overall survival (57.6 months for ADT+docetaxel) but does not provide a percentage OS rate at a specific time point for the overall treatment group.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median overall survival (44.0 months for ADT alone) but does not provide a percentage OS rate at a specific time point for the overall control group.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Control.evidence: No data are provided for OS rate specifically for patients with metachronous metastasis in the control arm.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median overall survival for high-volume disease (49.2 months for ADT+docetaxel) but does not provide a percentage OS rate at a specific time point.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: The document reports median overall survival for high-volume disease (32.2 months for ADT alone) but does not provide a percentage OS rate at a specific time point.
